[PDF_PAGE 38 | score=9] The largest of the living quarters are given to the large clanholds. These can number up to 20 rooms or more, and often lead to some unusual family blends. For example, if some young adults move out and a few of the elders pass away, a clan might recruit cousins, nephews, and even good friends to come in and join them. If they can’t find enough people to fill the place, they’ll be sent to live somewhere less spacious. THE F IREWELL This is the infernal heart of Thorin. It is the pit of magma that ignited the first forges of the dwarven city. Throughout the city’s his- tory, with its renewal each year at Balladine on the summer solstice, the firewell recharges the smelters and forgers, main- 
[PDF_PAGE 26 | score=7] 24 THE C ALNAR H ORSE chieftain and his loyal bodyguards (known as the Ten). The latter 11 horses each have The rise of the Calnar can be attributed to the maximum 40 hit points allowed for the many things. One of the most significant breed. was this powerful animal, domesticated and A Calnar horse trained as a warhorse car- bred to serve the needs of the Khalkist ries its rider into a lumbering charge. The dwarves. mount and rider do not travel as quickly as some lighter horses and their human riders, Armor Class: 7 but they can hit their targets with tremen- Movement: 12 dously crushing force. In melee, the horse Hit Dice: 5 stomps with its two forehooves (1-8 points THAC0: 15 of damage) a
[PDF_PAGE 69 | score=6] tamex, or unique spouts and cups that spill the liquid metal back and forth. Klar of all ages will sit and stare for hours at these dis- plays, which are commonly located in large, public chambers in the city. CREATURES OF T HORBARDIN RAPTOR B IRDS These great birds soar over the high peaks of Kharolis, making their nests among the in accessible reaches of the Anviltops. Throughout the centuries they have devel- oped a wary respect for the dwarves. It is a feeling that the inhabitants of Thorbardin return in equal measure. Already they loom like giants in domains. the dim recesses of these caverns, but they There are two primary types of fungifolk. are ready to lumber forward on command.
[PDF_PAGE 126 | score=3] 124 ZHAKAR L IZARDS These unique creatures provide a number of things to the dwarves of Zhakar, including food, transportation, and fighting ability. Most of the lizards are the fastclaws, but the largest and most powerful are trained as warclaws. [PDF_PAGE 194 | score=3] 62 DWARVES IN THE A GE OF D ARKNESS All of the dwarven realms suffered from the onslaught of the Cataclysm, as did every other nation on Ansalon. Where the wrack of the gods did not result in outright destruction, it took its toll in scars that would pervert attitudes and disrupt communication for centuries. The relatively light damage in Kayolin has already been described. The landslides of the Cataclysm carried away the approaches to Thor- bardin’s North Gate, but like Kayolin, the king- dom itself suffered little internal damage. How- ever, the most profound effect on that realm was from the tragedy of the Dwarfgate War. The realm of Thoradin conversely, was too close to the heart of the 
[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=2] CHISEL L OREMASTER , S UPREME However, when he is encountered on HISTORIAN OF THE D WARVES Krynn, Chisel most commonly appears as an old dwarf, so frail that he requires a Scion sturdy crutch just to hobble along. His gar- Strength: 12 ments are poor, and his possessions few. Dexterity: 11 Constitution: 12 History: Chisel was always determined to Intelligence: 20 record the story of the Smith-folk, for he Wisdom: 20 truly believed that they, and their descen- Charisma: 10 dants the dwarves, were the true salt of the Armor Class: 7 earth on Krynn. Born as a wise and intellec- THAC0: 17 tual Smith, he was changed to a Scion in Damage: 1d10 (or spell) the first blast of magic from the Graystone
[PDF_PAGE 25 | score=2] A GATE T HORWALLEN , F IRST C HIEFTAIN OF T HORIN 9th-Level Warrior Movement: Strength: Dexterity: Alignment: Constitution: IntelIigence: Wisdom: Charisma: Armor Class: THAC0: Damage: Hit points: Height: Weight: 18/00 15 6 18 12 Neutral Good 14 15 -3 5 1d10+10 83 4'3" 200 Ibs. Special Abilities: Infravision: 60’ Direction Sense: Thorwallen had an un- canny knack for recognizing directions and distances on the surface world. It is said that he never once got lost during the course of a lifetime’s exploration of new places. Equipment: Agate typically carried a sword, an axe, and a pick (each +4 equiva- lent). His full armor included a full-face helm, plate mail, and a battered shield (+4) hat
